title: Relative maxima and minima
description: Given the graph of a function, find all of its relative maximum and minimum points.
topic_path: Get ready for transformations of functions and modeling with functions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Mark all the relative *minimum* points in the graph.**

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints: A relative minimum point is a point that is lower than all other graph points around it. It's like the lowest point on a "valley."

The graph has three relative minimum points in the given section. One at $(-6,-7)$, one at $(-1,-2)$, and one at $(3,-2)$.



[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Mark all the relative *minimum* points in the graph.**

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints: A relative minimum point is a point that is lower than all other graph points around it. It's like the lowest point on a "valley."

The graph has two relative minimum points in the given section. One at $(-3,4)$, and one at $(5,-6)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
